19 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • An Innovative New Bonus Auction for Warrant Officers Army News 19 Feb 2026 -- The Army is launching a new Warrant Officer Retention Bonus Auction. This initiative introduces a market-based approach to retaining senior technical talent while ensuring responsible stewardship of taxpayer dollars. The program represents a shift from traditional, fixed-rate bonuses to a more flexible, market-driven system.
  • Inside how SETAF-AF will turn innovation into capability during African Lion 26 Army News 19 Feb 2026 -- More than 40 technology vendors will test cutting-edge military capabilities alongside U.S. military forces from April 20 to May 08, 2026, as part of an effort to close the gap between emerging innovation and the warfighter in Morocco during African Lion 26.
  • FSS Summit strengthens AFMC Force Support enterprise US Air Force 19 Feb 2026 -- Leaders across Air Force Materiel Command gathered for the inaugural AFMC Force Support Squadron Summit at the Air Force Institute of Technology, Feb. 10-11.
  • AFSOC strategizes on adapting special air warfare for an evolving world US Air Force 19 Feb 2026 -- Air Force Special Operations Command opened the 2026 Special Air Warfare Symposium with senior leaders underscoring the command's enduring focus on people and rapid adaptability to win the future fight in a complex and ever-changing global security environment.

  • Trump Slams UK Over Diego Garcia Lease Deal, Says Base Needed in Event of Iran Attack Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Wednesday sharply criticized the UK over a lease deal concerning Diego Garcia island, the site of a major US military base, saying it may be needed to counter a "potential attack" by Iran if nuclear talks collapse.

  • NATO's Allied Reaction Force exhibits Maritime Interdiction Operation capabilities off the northern coast of Germany NATO JFC Brunssum 19 Feb 2026 -- NATO Allied civilian and military leaders witnessed the Special Operations Maritime Task Unit (SOMTU) conduct a Maritime Interdiction Operation (MIO), from onboard the ESPS Castilla, on Wednesday, Feb. 18, 2026, during exercise STEADFAST DART 26.
  • Innovative Technologies put to the test during STEADFAST DART 26 NATO JFC Brunssum 19 Feb 2026 -- Turkish Armed Forces, which continue to develop and employ new technologies, exhibited the ASLAN Unmanned Ground Vehicle (UGV) during exercise STEADFAST DART 26 underway in northern Germany.

  • Syria Interested in Russia's Military Presence in Region - Lavrov Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- The Syrian government is interested in Russia maintaining its military presence in the region, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said.
  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov holds talks with Madagascar's Minister of Defence, Corps General Maminirina Ely Razafitombo Russia MoD 19 Feb 2026 -- The head of the Russian Defence Ministry noted that a lot of work had been done during the visit of the Malagasy delegation and, thanks to well-coordinated joint efforts, preparations for the meeting between the presidents of Russia and Madagascar had been completed.
  • Russian Deputy Defenсe Minister Vasily Osmakov holds meeting with First Deputy Minister of Revolutionary Armed Forces, Chief of General Staff of Revolutionary Armed Forces of Republic of Cuba, Army Corps General Roberto Sotolongo Russia MoD 19 Feb 2026 -- The parties discussed topical issues of military and military-technical cooperation. Further steps have been outlined for cooperation between the Russian Defence Ministry and the Cuban side.

  • New large support package to Ukraine focused on air defence Government Offices of Sweden 19 Feb 2026 -- Today, 19 February, the Government presented one of Sweden's largest ever military support packages for Ukraine.
  • After 4 years of war, the Netherlands continues to support Ukraine Netherlands Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Feb 2026 -- On 24 February 2022 Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Every day, the Ukrainian people fight for their lives and liberty - and for the security of Europe as a whole. Together with its allies, the Netherlands will continue to support Ukraine in defending itself against Russian aggression.
  • Ukraine says it upholds POW rights amid reported Filipino recruits Philippine News Agency 19 Feb 2026 -- The Ukrainian government is preserving the rights of all prisoners of war (POWs) in the ongoing conflict with Russia, its ambassador to the Philippines said Thursday, following reports that six Filipinos allegedly recruited as mercenaries for the Russian side are being held by Kyiv.
  • Ukraine's defence industry now meets over 50% of Defence Forces' needs and advances joint production with Europe Ukraine MOD 19 Feb 2026
  • Interceptors of jet Shaheds and cruise missiles: what makes AIM missiles stand out Ukraine MOD 19 Feb 2026

  • Australia conducts routine transit through South China Sea Australia DOD 19 Feb 2026 -- Royal Australian Navy Anzac Class Frigate HMAS Toowoomba has conducted a routine transit through the South China Sea in accordance with international law.
  • Defence tests cutting-edge autonomous capabilities during AUKUS Maritime Big Play Australia DOD 19 Feb 2026 -- Over 200 defence personnel, scientists, and industry participants from Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States came together on Australia's east coast to test cutting-edge autonomous capabilities as part of an AUKUS Pillar II Maritime Big Play exercise.

  • Enhanced Canadian Armed Forces Arctic operations reinforce sovereignty throughout 2026 Canada 19 Feb 2026 -- Throughout 2026, the Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) will conduct operations across the Arctic and the North to detect, deter, and, if required, defend against threats—demonstrating its capability and capacity to protect Canadian sovereignty across the land, maritime, air, cyber, and space domains.

  • DRDO conducts successful qualification test for Gaganyaan Drogue Parachute India PIB 19 Feb 2026 -- India's human spaceflight programme has achieved a key milestone with the successful qualification level load test of Drogue Parachute for Gaganyaan programme at Rail Track Rocket Sled (RTRS) facility of Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) at Terminal Ballistics Research Laboratory (TBRL), Chandigarh
  • MILAN 2026: Raksha Mantri interacts with visiting Navy Chiefs and Naval delegations from nine ASEAN member states India PIB 19 Feb 2026 -- On the sidelines of the MILAN 2026 Naval exercise, Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held an interaction with visiting Navy Chiefs and Naval delegations from nine ASEAN member states in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh on February 19, 2026
  • Global fraternity must join hands to tackle evolving maritime challenges, says Raksha Mantri as he inaugurates Exercise MILAN in Vizag India PIB 19 Feb 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh has called upon the international community to effectively tackle the evolving complex & interconnected challenges at sea while acting in the spirit of mutual respect and give & take

  • Iran, Russia Hold Naval Drills Amid US Military Build Up In Arabian Sea RFE/RL 19 Feb 2026 -- Iran and Russia launched joint naval drills in Iran's southern waters on February 19, as the United States continues expanding its military presence in the Arabian Sea.
  • Joint Iran-Russia naval drills come to an end IRNA 19 Feb 2026 -- The joint naval exercise of the Islamic Republic of Iran Navy and the Russian Federation Navy has concluded with the departure of the Russian Navy's helicopter carrier
  • Iranian, Russian naval forces rescue hijacked ship in mock operation during naval drills Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- Iranian and Russian forces have successfully performed a mock operation to rescue a hijacked vessel during combined naval drills.

  • Remarks by PM Netanyahu at the IDF Bahad 1 Cadets Graduation Ceremony Israel - Prime Minister 19 Feb 2026

  • Estonia Procures Hundreds of Bunkers for the Baltic Defence Line Estonia CDI 19 Feb 2026 -- The Estonian Centre for Defence Investments (ECDI) has announced a public procurement for the large-scale acquisition of bunkers. Estonia intends to procure up to 600 concrete bunkers for deployment along its eastern border. Ministry of Defence of Latvia has also expressed interest in this procurement and is currently discussing the potential involvement.

  • Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen's speech at the Bundesverband der Deutschen Industrie event in Münich Finnish MoD 19 Feb 2026 -- Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen held a speech at the Bundesverband der Deutschen Industrie event in Münich on 13 February 2026.

  • Philippines, United States Reaffirm Strong Alliance, Advance Shared Priorities at the 12th Bilateral Strategic Dialogue in Manila Philippines DFA 19 Feb 2026 -- The Republic of the Philippines and the United States of America reaffirmed their enduring alliance at the 12th Philippines-United States Bilateral Strategic Dialogue (BSD), held in Manila on 16 February 2026.
  • Canada, PH eye military intel-sharing deal Philippine News Agency 19 Feb 2026 -- Canada and the Philippines are in active talks on a military intelligence-sharing agreement, Canadian Ambassador to the Philippines David Hartman said Thursday.
  • Navy chief hails French partners for help in PH naval drills Philippine News Agency 19 Feb 2026 -- Philippine Navy (PN) chief Vice Admiral Jose Ma. Ambrosio Ezpeleta has expressed his thanks to the French Navy for its continued support and participation in the Philippines' maritime activities and exercises.
  • 8 jets from S. Korea, 4 from PH join forces for 'Friendship Flight' Philippine News Agency 19 Feb 2026 -- Around eight Republic of Korea Air Force (ROKAF) aircraft from its "Black Eagles" Team and four from the Philippine Air Force (PAF) will be taking part in the Feb. 23 scheduled "Friendship Flight" which aims to boost interoperability between the two services and highlights the growing defense ties between the two nations.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Vietnam Foreign Minister Trung US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with UAE Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Paraguayan President Peña US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Czech Foreign Minister Macinka US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Feb. 19, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 19 February 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 19 Feb 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • FM Sa'ar participates in a high-level ministerial session of the UN Security Council on the Middle East Israel MFA 19 Feb 2026 -- FM Sa'ar at the UN Security Council: "Jewish presence in the Land of Israel has never ceased, for even a day. This is probably the clearest case of proven and documented historical rights of any nation to any land."
  • FM Sa'ar's press statement at the UN ahead of today's High-Level Security Council Session on the Middle East Israel MFA 19 Feb 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: "Yesterday, 85 countries stood here and denied the Jewish people's right to live in the same places recognized as belonging to a Jewish national home. Amazingly, so many countries say that Jewish presence in our ancient homeland violates international law. The opposite is true: No other nation, in any other place in the world, has a stronger right than our historical and documented right to the land of the Bible."

  • Hamas condemns Trump's 'Board of Peace' meeting, calls for immediate halt to Israeli aggression Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has strongly condemned the convening of Gaza's so-called Board of Peace in the United States.

  • Fears of ethnic cleansing in Gaza and the West Bank: UN rights report UN News 19 Feb 2026 -- Increased Israeli attacks and the forced transfer of Palestinians have sparked concern over ethnic cleansing in the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report issued on Thursday.
  • Ethnic cleansing concerns in Gaza and West Bank amid intensified violence and forcible transfers by Israel - UN report OHCHR 19 Feb 2026 -- A UN Human Rights Office reportreleased today raises concerns over ethnic cleansing by Israeli authorities in both Gaza and the West Bank, amid increased attacks and forcible transfers that appear aimed at a permanent displacement of Palestinians throughout the occupied territories.
  • Israel's West Bank land grab 'will consolidate annexation', warns UN rapporteur Albanese Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- The UN special rapporteur for the occupied Palestinian territories has cautioned that Israel's decision to extend land expropriation in the occupied West Bank could solidify annexation, possibly constituting aggression under international law, and deepen what the world's highest court has classified as an illegal occupation.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Board of Peace Event US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026
  • US Plans to Build 5,000-Person Military Base in Gaza Strip - Reports Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump plans to establish a military base in the Gaza Strip for 5,000 personnel, the Guardian reported on Thursday, citing Board of Peace contracting documents.
  • Rubio at Board of Peace Meeting Says 'Plan B' for Gaza Would Mean Return to War Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned on Thursday that there is no such thing as "Plan B" for peace in Gaza, as it would mean going back to an armed conflict.
  • Trump Says Washington Will Contribute $10Bln to Board of Peace Gaza Reconstruction Effort Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- The United States will contribute $10 billion to the Board of Peace reconstruction efforts for Gaza, President Donald Trump said on Thursday.

  • Iran condemns Israeli regime's seizure of Palestinian lands in West Bank IRNA 19 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has condemned the Israeli regime's recent decision to confiscate and register Palestinian lands in the West Bank as "state lands" under its sovereignty.
  • Iran slams Israel's West Bank land grab as extension of genocide against Palestinians Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- Iran has denounced Israel's new land grab scheme in the occupied West Bank as an extension of the occupying regime's policy of genocide against the Palestinian people.

  • The Prime Minister participates in the inaugural session of the Peace Council Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 19 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan participated in the inaugural session of the Peace Council in Washington.
  • His Majesty King Mohammed VI, may God assist Him, backs the actions of U.S. President Donald Trump for the reconstruction of Gaza and calls for the launch of a genuine peace process in the Middle East, stated the Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates, MFA Nasser Bourita, on Thursday in Washington, D.C. Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Feb 2026
  • Statement by H.E. Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar, Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of Pakistan, At the United Nations Security Council Briefing on "The Middle East, including the Palestinian Question" (18 February 2026) Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Feb 2026 -- I thank you, Madam President, for convening this important meeting. It is a pleasure to see you chairing today's very important discussion. I also thank Under Secretary-General Rosemary DiCarlo for her sobering briefing.
  • Without Palestinian State, There Will Be No Peace, Stability in Middle East - Lavrov Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- Russia is convinced that without a Palestinian state, there will be no peace or stability in the Middle East, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan UNOCHA 19 Feb 2026

  • PH addressing persistent cyber attacks tied to WPS dispute Philippine News Agency 19 Feb 2026 -- A ranking official of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Thursday said the country is facing "persistent cyber attacks" linked to its territorial dispute with China regarding the West Philippine Sea (WPS).

  • On night 18/19 February 2026, Security Forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Lakki Marwat District, on reported presence of khwarij belonging to Indian Proxy, Fitna al Khwarij. ISPR Pakistan 19 Feb 2026

  • Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Sudan by Ms. Edem Wosornu, Director, Crisis Response Division, OCHA, on behalf of Tom Fletcher, USG and Emergency Relief Coordinator UNOCHA 19 Feb 2026
  • Entering Fourth Year, Sudan's Brutal Civil War Hits 'Horrific Milestone', Speakers Tell Security Council, Citing Rising Sexual Violence, Mass Atrocities UN Security Council 19 Feb 2026 -- As Sudan marks "1,000 days of a brutal war," the Security Council heard today that women and girls are enduring horrific sexual violence, as a civil society briefer issued a blunt warning: "Unless you act now, you will have more blood on your hands."
  • Sudan: 'Hallmarks of genocide' found in El Fasher, UN investigators detail mass killings and ethnic targeting UN News 19 Feb 2026 -- The Rapid Support Forces (RSF) carried out ethnically targeted killings, widespread sexual violence and enforced disappearances during their late-October takeover of El Fasher in Sudan's Darfur region — acts that a UN fact-finding mission said show "hallmarks of genocide" against the Zaghawa and Fur communities and signal an ongoing risk of further atrocities.
  • 'No corner of Sudan is safe': UN officials warn of famine and atrocities as war intensifies UN News 19 Feb 2026 -- Nearly three years into Sudan's war, violence is intensifying, famine conditions are looming in parts of the country, and civilians remain trapped between shifting front lines, senior UN political and humanitarian officials told the Security Council on Thursday, warning that the risk of further mass atrocities remains alarmingly high.
  • Sudan: Evidence in El-Fasher reveals genocidal campaign, targeting non-Arab communities, UN Fact-Finding Mission says OHCHR 19 Feb 2026 -- The Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan said in a new report today that the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) carried out a coordinated campaign of destruction against non-Arab communities in and around El-Fasher, the hallmarks of which point to genocide. While the Mission documented war crimes and crimes against humanity, the evidence establishes that at least three underlying acts of genocide were committed.
  • EU Statement -- UN Security Council: Meeting on Sudan Delegation of the European Union to the United Nations in New York 19 Feb 2026
  • Treasury Sanctions Sudanese Paramilitary Commanders for Atrocities in El-Fasher US Treasury 19 Feb 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned three commanders of the Rapid Support Forces (RSF)—a Sudanese paramilitary group—for their actions in El-Fasher, Sudan. These individuals were involved in the RSF's 18-month siege of and eventual capture of El-Fasher, in which the RSF perpetrated a horrific campaign of ethnic killings, torture, starvation, and sexual violence. Since the beginning of Sudan's civil war in April 2023, the RSF and its aligned militias have committed widespread atrocities, including war crimes, crimes against humanity, and genocide.
  • Foreign Secretary's statement on the UN Fact Finding Mission Report on El Fasher UK FCDO 19 Feb 2026 -- Statement by Yvette Cooper, Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, on the UN fact finding mission report on El Fasher.
  • Let this be the time that the world comes together to end the cycle of bloodshed in Sudan: UK Statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 19 Feb 2026 -- Statement by The Rt Hon Yvette Cooper, Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, at the UN Security Council meeting on Sudan.
  • Joint Ministerial Statement on Protection of Civilians and Humanitarian Operations in Sudan UK FCDO 19 Feb 2026 -- Joint Ministerial Statement from the UK and partners on the Protection of Civilians and Humanitarian Operations in Sudan

  • WFP intensifies emergency response as conflict escalates in Jonglei, South Sudan WFP 19 Feb 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is scaling up its emergency response to reach the growing number of people forced to flee spiralling conflict in South Sudan's Jonglei state. These life-saving food distributions, transported along river routes, come amid increasing insecurity that is hampering humanitarian efforts and threatening to drive hunger in the region to unprecedented levels.

News Reports

  • Ranking Member Shaheen Statement on Arrest of Journalists Reporting on Third-Country Deportations to Cameroon US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 19 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statement after reports that journalists and a lawyer were detained by Cameroonian authorities while investigating U.S. third-country deportations:

  • Latvia: Progress in anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing, and areas for improvement Council of Europe 19 Feb 2026 -- The Council of Europe's anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing monitoring body, MONEYVAL, has published a report assessing the effectiveness of Latvia's measures against money laundering, countering terrorist financing and proliferation financing and their level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force Recommendations, at the time of the on-site visit conducted in November 2024.

  • Sanctioning CJNG-Controlled Timeshare Resort for Defrauding U.S. Citizens US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026 -- The United States is sanctioning Kovay Gardens, a Mexican timeshare resort, along with five Mexican individuals and 17 Mexican companies linked to timeshare fraud schemes orchestrated by the Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG).
  • Treasury Targets Cartel-Linked Timeshare Resort Defrauding U.S. Citizens US Treasury 19 Feb 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned a timeshare fraud network led by the terrorist Cartel de Jalisco Nueva Generacion (CJNG). The action targets Kovay Gardens, a Mexican timeshare resort, as well as five Mexican individuals and 17 Mexican companies associated with the network.

  • Meeting of judges of courts of general jurisdiction, military, and arbitration courts President of Russia 19 Feb 2026 -- The President took part in the annual meeting of judges of courts of general jurisdiction, military and arbitration courts of the Russian Federation.
  • Russian-Malagasy talks President of Russia 19 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin met with Michael Randrianirina, President for the Re-Foundation of the Republic of Madagascar, at the Kremlin today. The Malagasy leader is in Russia on an official visit.
  • Will EU Sanctions On Russia Stall As Hungary And Slovakia Leverage Oil Vetoes? RFE/RL 19 Feb 2026 -- As the European Union races to approve its new sanctions package on Russia by the fourth anniversary of Moscow's full-scale invasion of Ukraine next week, a new obstacle may have appeared.

  • No matter what pretext Japan uses to exercise 'collective self-defense' to intervene in Taiwan question, China will resolutely respond: Chinese envoy to UN Global Times 19 Feb 2026 -- No matter what pretext Japan uses to exercise "collective self-defense" to intervene in the Taiwan question, it would constitute aggression against China, and China will resolutely respond, China's permanent representative to the United Nations Fu Cong said on Wednesday local time.
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PLA Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 19 Feb 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 19 Feb 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Groundbreaking Ceremony of Fifth-stage Construction Project in Hwasong Area KCNA 19 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the groundbreaking ceremony of the fifth-stage construction project in the Hwasong area on Wednesday.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Ceremony of Presenting Military Hardware KCNA 19 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at a ceremony of presenting military hardware on Wednesday.
  • Ceremony of Presenting 600mm-Calibre Multiple Rocket Launchers Held in DPRK KCNA 19 Feb 2026 -- The workers in the munitions industry of the DPRK is providing a sure guarantee for the continuous evolution and radical development of the self-defensive capability, giving a boost to invincibility and mightiness of our state in the outpost of the national defence industry revolution in the new era energetically led by the great Workers' Party of Korea. In this course, they presented 600mm-calibre multiple rocket launchers, carrying their spirit of devotedly implementing the Party's policies and fervent patriotism and loyalty, to the Ninth Congress of the WPK.
  • Press Statement of Kim Yo Jong, Vice Department Director of C.C., WPK KCNA 19 Feb 2026 -- Kim Yo Jong, vice department director of the Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea, released the following press statement on February 18:

  • Republic of Korea: EU-Republic of Korea High-Level Political Dialogue takes place in Seoul European External Action Service (EEAS) 19 Feb 2026 -- The 9th High-Level Political Dialogue between the European Union (EU) and the Republic of Korea took place on 19 February in Seoul. Together with Ms Chung Eui-Hae, Deputy Minister for Political Affairs of the Republic of Korea, Deputy Secretary General for Political Affairs Olof Skoog, co-chaired the Dialogue.
  • Revival of 2018 inter-Korean military accord eyes no-fly zones KOREA.net 19 Feb 2026 -- Minister of Unification Chung Dongyoung on Feb. 18 announced a preemptive review and promotion of the restoration of parts of a 2018 military agreement with North Korea, including the designation of no-fly zones, "to prevent accidental clashes and build military trust."

  • Diplomacy Or Conflict? US-Iran Crisis Now '50-50,' Experts Say RFE/RL 19 Feb 2026 -- After US-Iran nuclear talks concluded in Geneva under heavy American pressure on Tehran to curb its nuclear activities, leading Washington experts say the crisis now stands at a tipping point, with the chances of peace or war at "50-50."
  • Trump Says Clarity Around Iran Nuclear Negotiations Will Likely be Known in Next 10 Days Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- The United States will likely be getting more clarity around the negotiations on Iran's nuclear program in the next ten days, President Donald Trump said on Thursday.
  • US Military Poised for Iran Strike, Awaiting Trump's Final Call - Report Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- Top national security officials have told Donald Trump the US military could be ready to launch strikes on Iran as early as Saturday, reports CBS News.
  • EU hails progress in Iran-US talks, warns against military escalation in West Asia IRNA 19 Feb 2026 -- Anouar El Anouni, a spokesperson for the European Commission has warned that any US attack on Iran would have damaging impacts on the stability of the West Asia region, voicing support for the ongoing negotiations between Tehran and Washington.
  • IAEA Upholds Iran-US Negotiations to Avoid Use of Force - Director General Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- The current objective of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) is to create conditions for a possible agreement between Iran and the United States in a bid to avoid the use of force, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi said on Thursday.
  • Any new US strike on Iran is 'playing with fire', Russia's Lavrov warns Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov has warned that any new US strike on Iran is "playing with fire" and will have "serious consequences."
  • EU terrorist list: Council designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist organisation EC/CEU 19 Feb 2026 -- Following the political agreement reached by the Foreign Affairs Council on 29 January, the Council formally decided today to add the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) of Iran to the EU terrorist list.
  • Iran and Saudi FMs discuss outcomes of latest indirect Iran-US talks Press TV 19 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has held a telephone conversation with his Saudi counterpart, Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud.
  • UNICEF calls for the release of all children arrested in the latest public unrest in Iran UNICEF 19 Feb 2026 -- Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa Edouard Beigbeder
  • UNICEF calls for release of children detained during Iran protests UN News 19 Feb 2026 -- The UN Children's Fund (UNICEF) has called for the immediate release of all children detained during the recent public unrest in Iran, expressing deep concern that minors arrested in connection with the protests remain behind bars.

  • Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Concludes One-Day Official Visit to New York Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Feb 2026 -- Deputy Prime Minister/Minister of Foreign Minister of Pakistan, Senator Mohammad Ishaq Dar, concluded a busy and productive one-day official visit to New York on 18 February 2026. During the visit, he participated in a High-Level meeting of the UN Security Council on Palestine, chaired by the UK Foreign Secretary, ahead of the inaugural meeting of the Board of Peace (BoP). He also held a series of bilateral meetings and other official engagements.
  • Transcript of the Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday 19th February 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 19 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Pakistan Prime Minister Sharif US Dept. of State 19 Feb 2026

  • Тelephone conversation with President of Belarus Alexander Lukashenko President of Russia 19 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of Belarus Alexander Lukashenko.
  • Belarusian Former Presidential Candidate Mikalay Statkevich Freed From Prison After Stroke RFE/RL 19 Feb 2026 -- Belarusian former presidential candidate Mikalay Statkevich, who has been imprisoned for over 10 years, was released to his home in Minsk after having a stroke in prison, according to his family.

  • Cuba to Defend Sovereignty While Being Open to Dialogue - Foreign Minister Sputnik 19 Feb 2026 -- Cuba will resolutely defend its independence while maintaining readiness for an equal dialogue with any country in the world, Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez Parrilla said on Thursday.

  • "Türkiye takes any kind of steps for its own security" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 19 Feb 2026 -- Speaking at an iftar dinner with martyrs' families, President Erdoğan said: "Today Türkiye takes any kind of steps for its own security not only inside but also beyond its borders. Wherever there is a threat or danger, we neutralize it at its source without seeking permission from anyone."

  • Press remarks by President António Costa at the press conference following the meeting with Prime Minister of Norway, Jonas Gahr Støre EC/CEU 19 Feb 2026

  • The President met with politicians in charge of implementing foreign and national security policy President of the Republic of Lithuania 19 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with politicians responsible for implementing foreign and national security policy. The meeting focused on Lithuania's positions on key foreign policy and national security issues.

  • World News in Brief: Conflict deepens hunger crisis in South Sudan, restrictions hinder aid delivery in Gaza, UN child rights envoy concludes first visit to Syria UN News 19 Feb 2026 -- The UN World Food Programme (WFP) is scaling up its emergency response in South Sudan's Jonglei state, where escalating conflict has displaced hundreds of thousands of people and pushed hunger to critical levels.
